summary: Persistent PR-review bookmarks via SessionStart hook

message.content: I keep getting interrupted when reviewing PRs because every Claude Code session starts fresh — no memory of what I was doing yesterday. I want a workflow where the session can resume the actual review state, not just the conversation history.

content.text:
That's a real productivity tax — context-switching cost compounds when each rehydration is manual. Three options stacked from cheapest to most reliable:

**Option 1: PR-context bookmarks.** A `/bookmark` slash command that writes the current PR url, files you've touched, and a short note into `.claude/bookmarks.json`. Cheap, but you still have to ask Claude to load it next session.

**Option 2: Auto-context-on-resume.** Same bookmarks file, but the session start hook reads the most recent bookmark and pre-loads it into the first turn. You walk in and Claude already knows where you were.

**Option 3: Persistent task list.** A TODO file scoped per-repo. Survives sessions, syncs across devices if the repo is.

Which fits your workflow? I'd start with Option 2 — biggest payoff for the least ceremony.

message.content: Option 2 sounds right. Show me the hook.

content.text: Here's the minimal version. Two files: a hook that fires on SessionStart and a helper that records bookmarks.
Now register the hook in `.claude/settings.json` so the harness actually runs it:

Done. To save a bookmark mid-review, just tell me "bookmark this" and I'll write to `.claude/bookmarks.json` with the PR URL, the files we touched, and a one-line note. Next time you start a session here, the hook reads the latest entry and you walk in with context already loaded — no manual reload, no re-explaining what you were doing.

One caveat: the hook only loads bookmarks newer than 24h. Older than that and you probably want a clean slate anyway. Easy to tweak the threshold in `session-start.js` if you disagree.

message.content: Perfect. This is going to save me 10 min every morning.

content.text: That's the real win — not the lines of code, but the friction removed. If you find yourself wanting the same pattern for other resumable workflows (incident response, sprint reviews, on-call), the hook generalizes: just write different bookmark types and switch on the first one's `kind`. Holler if you want me to extend it.
